summary: A legendary early‑Dead audience tape that delivers a powerful set – highlighted by an epic Dark Star and flawless transitions – with surprisingly crystal‑clear sound for a crowd recording.
review: Listeners repeatedly hail this show as one of the all‑time greatest Dead performances, pointing to the soaring Dark Star, the immaculate Sugar Magnolia segue and the magical Attics moment.  Though it’s an audience (AUD) source, the tape is praised as exceptionally clear and vivid, with only minor imperfections that never detract from the music.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive comments on both performance and audio; most reviewers award 5‑star ratings, with only a few mixed notes about minor recording flaws.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Audience tape captured by Ken’s team; praised for clarity, presence of crowd excitement and minimal drop‑outs. A few reviewers note it isn’t a perfect soundboard mix, but compared to other AUDs it is among the best.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows seamlessly with tight NFA jams, brilliant Dark Star weaving, a flawless Sugar Magnolia transition and an unforgettable Attics segment; reviewers highlight the band’s telepathic interplay and lock‑tight rhythm section.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s lead work is described as transcendent – “break‑neck speed”, “channeling another world” and a perfect tone throughout.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass is noted for locking in with the drums, providing a solid foundation for the improvisations.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir receives praise for his rhythmic drive and vocal interplay, especially during the NFA sections.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum lock is repeatedly highlighted as “solid”, “tight” and integral to the show’s power.
